description: Applies §8.1's version acceptance and refusal rules: a tool interprets a document only when the declared version belongs to its explicit supported set, and produces a version refusal otherwise — in either direction, above or below that set. Build metadata is ignored in comparison; a prerelease is not accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease. Supporting one version implies nothing about any other: a tool that accepts a release line does so by its own support declaration, never by an inference this specification imposes.
notes: Tests assume the SDK under test supports a version range bounded below `999.x` for major-version refusal cases, and bounded below `0.999.x` for pre-1.0 minor-version refusal cases. SDK conformance to OBI-T-04 is independent of which specific version the SDK supports; the refusal tests assert behavior on documents declaring versions outside any plausible SDK's supported set in this version family. Downward refusal (a document declaring a version below the SDK's supported set MUST be refused rather than processed under the wrong rules) is fixtured with `requiresMinSupported`: those tests apply only to SDKs whose MinSupportedVersion is at or above the annotation's value, and are skipped (reported separately) for SDKs whose set extends down to the document's version. Acceptance-presuming positives are gated with `requiresSupports` (the acceptance-gate annotation; see the corpus README): each is administered only to tools whose OBI-T-04 acceptance predicate accepts the annotation's version, because which versions a tool accepts is its own support declaration (§8.1), never a corpus assumption — a conformant tool supporting exactly {0.2.0} must refuse a 0.2.1 document, and gating on the tested-range declarations instead would make the reference SDKs (which accept 0.2.1 via their own release-line declaration) skip tests they pass. The current-version and build-metadata cases carry `requiresSupports: "0.2.0"`; the higher-patch case carries `requiresSupports: "0.2.1"`, so for a tool that has declared a release line it checks the document-processing path honors that declaration end-to-end. The prerelease case stays ungated: a prerelease, which sorts below its release, MUST NOT be accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (`0.2.0-rc.1` asserts default rejection by a tool that has not opted in; the opt-in exception is not separately fixtured).

tests.description: document declares the current spec version (administered only to tools whose acceptance predicate accepts 0.2.0)
build metadata is ignored in comparison; `0.2.0+build.1` is treated as `0.2.0` (administered only to tools whose acceptance predicate accepts 0.2.0)
a tool that has declared support for the release line accepts a higher patch within it (administered only to tools whose acceptance predicate accepts 0.2.1)
a prerelease is not accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (default rejection)
document declares a major version higher than any plausible SDK supports
document declares a far-future major version with non-zero minor
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor than any plausible SDK's supported set
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor with a patch component
post-1.0: document declares a same-major-higher-minor version (administered only to tools whose acceptance predicate accepts it)
downward refusal: pre-1.0 document declares a lower minor outside the SDK's supported set (refused rather than processed under the wrong rules)
downward refusal: a higher patch on an unsupported lower minor is still outside the set (patch never rescues a refused minor)
